title: toxicodendron hirtellum var. microcarpum
intro: Toxicodendron hirtellum var. microcarpum, also known as the dwarf poison oak, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its shiny, lobed leaves and yellowish-green color. This plant contains urushiol, a potent allergenic and irritant compound, but it also possesses medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The dwarf poison oak has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
